songs.title: G-G-Go!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
G! G! G-g-g!
(g… g… g…)

[Hook]
G! G! G-g-g!
What begins with G?
G-g-goat! G-g-goat!
It's a goat! (a goat!)

[Verse 2]
G! G! G-g-g!
What begins with G?
G-g-goose! G-g-goose!
It's a goose! (a goose!)

[Verse 3]
G! G! G-g-g!
What begins with G?
G-g-guitar! G-g-guitar!
It's a guitar! (a guitar!)

[Verse 4]
G! G! G-g-g!
What begins with G?
G-g-gift! G-g-gift!
It's a gift! (a gift!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not G!
(g… g… g…)

[Outro]
G-g-go! Go, dog, go!
The dog digs! (dig, dig, dig!)
G! G! G-g-g!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The G sound is a hard voiced stop /g/ made deep in the throat — NEVER 'gee' (letter name), NEVER 'guh' (schwa), and NEVER the soft /j/ of 'giraffe'. The '(g… g… g…)' lines are short dry voiced clicks on the beat. All basket words use the HARD g (goat, goose, guitar, gift). Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame; the outro hands off to the dog for the word song.

songs.title: The Dog Digs
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(dig, dig, dig…)

[Hook]
The dog digs! The dog digs!
What is in the pit?
A pig! (a pig?!)
The dog got a pig!

[Verse 2]
The dog digs! The dog digs!
What is in the pit?
A pot! (a pot?!)
The dog got a pot!

[Verse 3]
The dog digs! The dog digs!
What is in the pit?
A hat! (a hat?!)
The dog got a hat!

[Verse 4]
The dog digs! The dog digs!
What is in the pit?
A cat?! (a CAT?!)
The cat is mad! (hiss!)

[Outro]
The dog digs. The dog digs…
What is in the pit?
(POTATO!)
The dog got a potato!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ONE frame: 'The dog got a ___.' — one word swaps per verse. The class shouts the surprise noun in the (…?!) echoes. Dog just joined the cast — this is his anthem. The potato is the final dig; land it loud after the whispered 'The dog digs…'.

spreads.artNote: A scruffy little dog mid-dig, dirt flying, tail up, delighted. The Dog's first solo book — introduce him as the class hero.
The dog deeper in a fresh hole, only ears and paws visible, dirt spraying up.
The dog has unearthed a startled pink pig sitting in the hole. Absurd, deadpan. Page-turn reveal.
Even deeper hole, the pig now watching from the rim, unimpressed.
The dog pulls a battered cooking pot out of the hole, ears flopped, proud.
A hat now perched on the dog's head, tipped over one eye. The pile of dug-up things grows beside the hole.
The dog has dug up the Cat, who is furious, fur on end, ears flat. Cast callback — the Cat did NOT want to be buried treasure.
Suspense spread. The dog digging slowly, tongue out, dramatic side-light, something round beginning to show at the bottom.
THE POTATO, spotlit at the bottom of the hole, wearing its tiny round glasses, utterly serene. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. The dog stares.

extras: Moveable alphabet: build d-o-g, p-i-g, d-i-g, g-o-t, and flip d-i-g → g-o-t to hear the g move
Sandpaper letter g: the hard /g/ formed low in the throat — hum it against the throat, never 'guh'
Sound sort: /g/ words vs the potato (initial-sound I Spy with the sound basket)
Dog joins the cast — welcome-the-Dog circle; add his figure to the story shelf beside Cat and Ant

teacherFocus: Welcome the Dog to the cast — he is the digger, the doer, the class's own hero. Teach the HARD /g/ made low in the throat (never 'guh'). Blend d-o-g, p-i-g, d-i-g, g-o-t on the moveable alphabet, and show the g migrate: d-i-g flips to g-o-t. The book hands the class a running joke — the dog digs up sillier and sillier things — and every child reads it themselves.
